summary: Solid test suite covering all core requirements (manifest parsing, license checking, report generation) with good mocking and meaningful test names. However, edge case coverage is limited: tests verify only 3 version specifiers (==, >=, ~=) when implementation supports 6+ operators; no tests for malformed JSON/YAML, special characters in package names, or config validation errors. Test design could be more DRY with pytest fixtures for repeated config/dependency setup. The error-handling test is weak—only checks FileNotFoundError is raised, not error message quality. Would catch basic regressions but miss real-world edge cases and robustness issues.
